NEW_ZEALAND_RISK_ACKNOWLEDGEMENT.title: Risk Acknowledgment for New Zealand
description: Please inform the student of the following risks associated with financial sponsorship:

list: **Sponsor Requirements:** Immigration New Zealand (INZ) strongly prefers sponsors who are close family relatives (parents, siblings).

**Higher-Risk Sponsors:** If the sponsor is a distant relative, family friend, or non-family member, the visa application carries a **significantly higher risk of refusal**. INZ will heavily scrutinize the sponsor's motives and the genuineness of their support.
**Required Documents:** The sponsor must provide extensive evidence of their financial capacity and their relationship to the student. This will be detailed in a later step.

description: Does the student understand and agree to proceed despite these risks?

NEW_ZEALAND_VISA_REQUIREMENT_INFO.title: Important Visa Requirement Information
description: Please inform the student that an **official English test (such as IELTS or PTE) is a mandatory requirement** for most New Zealand student visa applications. It is crucial to set the expectation that they will likely need to take one of these tests.
While some institutions may waive the English test for admission, the **embassy often requires it for the visa itself**. Please advise the student accordingly.

NZ_VISA_PAYMENT_INFO.title: New Zealand Visa Fee Payment
description: Please set the expectation that the **visa application fee** and any associated VFS Global service fees must be paid at the time of lodging the visa application.

The **tuition fee**, however, is typically paid only after the visa application has been **'Approved in Principle' (AIP)**. This protects the student's funds, as the bulk of the payment is made only after INZ has indicated they are likely to grant the visa.

NZ_PATHWAY_INFO.title: Pathway Student Visa Information
description: A **Pathway Student Visa** allows the student to undertake up to **three consecutive courses** on a single visa with one or more approved education providers in New Zealand.
**Benefits:**

list: **Convenience:** No need to apply for a new visa for each course.
**Certainty:** Provides a clear, long-term study plan.
**Work Rights:** Standard student work rights apply throughout the pathway.

description: Please ensure that the first course in the pathway starts within 3 months of the visa being granted.
For more information, visit the [official INZ page](https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/visas/visa/pathway-student-visa).

NZ_FEE_PAYING_INFO.title: Fee Paying Student Visa
description: This is the standard visa for international students planning to study full-time in New Zealand for a single course.
**Key Requirements:**

list: **Offer of Place:** An acceptance letter from an approved New Zealand education provider.
**Proof of Funds:** Evidence that the student can cover their tuition fees and living expenses.
**Health & Character:** Meet standard health and character requirements.
**Bona Fide:** The student must be a genuine student who intends to leave New Zealand after their studies.

description: For more details, visit the [official INZ page](https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/visas/visa/fee-paying-student-visa).

NZ_LOWER_QUALIFICATION_RISK.title: Warning: Qualification Downgrade Risk
description: Choosing a lower-level course (e.g., a Level 5 Diploma) after completing a higher qualification (like a Bachelor's Degree) can be a **major red flag** for Immigration New Zealand.
**Why it's a risk:** The visa officer may question if the student is a 'Bona Fide' (genuine) student and might suspect their primary motivation is to work, not study.

**To proceed, the student must have a very strong and logical reason** for this 'downgrade,' which must be explained clearly in their Statement of Purpose. For example, are they making a career change that requires this specific foundational course?
Does the student understand this risk and wish to continue?

NZ_POST_STUDY_WORK_VISA.title: Post-Study Work Visa Information
description: The Post-Study Work Visa allows students who have completed an acceptable New Zealand qualification to work for any employer in New Zealand.
**Visa Length:**
The length of the visa is **equal to the duration of the student's study** in New Zealand, up to a maximum of **3 years**.
**Key Eligibility:**

list: The student must have completed a New Zealand qualification at **Level 7 or above** (e.g., Bachelor's Degree, Graduate Diploma, Master Degree, PhD).
For non-degree Level 7 qualifications, the course must be on the **Green List**.
The student must apply within **3 months** of their student visa expiring.

description: For full eligibility details, please visit the [official INZ website](https://www.immigration.govt.nz/new-zealand-visas/visas/visa/post-study-work-visa).
